mnemonics: S.K.I.P. - Similarity, Kernel, Instance, Prototype - the core concepts of similarity-based learning
keyPoints: Learning is based on comparing new instances to stored examples using a similarity or distance metric.
It is often 'lazy' (instance-based), meaning no explicit model is built during training.
Common algorithms include k-Nearest Neighbors (k-NN) and Radial Basis Function (RBF) networks.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to normalize features, causing features with larger scales to dominate the distance calculation.
Using k=1 (nearest neighbor) which makes the model highly sensitive to noise and outliers in the training data.
Confusing similarity-based methods with parametric methods that create an explicit model (e.g., linear regression).
